checks.a_analysis: The conversion results correspond directly to text found in the source PDF. While some titles like '20200522 Board Agenda 685F9Ca8' are likely system-generated for metadata, no body text or agenda items have been hallucinated.
The phone number (984-204-1487) and Conference ID (766 174 851#) provided for the Microsoft Teams Meeting in the output exactly match the source document.

All major high-level sections (Items 1 through 13) are represented in the output. While specific sub-content is missing (addressed in C5), no entire pages or top-level agenda items were silently dropped.

The source document's main agenda table contains 9 data rows (Call to Order, SL 2020-3, Roll Call, and items 1-6). The output JSON table (reading_order 4) contains 9 data rows corresponding to these items. Although some columns have been merged into single cells during OCR, the row count is accurate.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are two major omissions of content within sections. First, Item 5 (Consent Agenda) in the source contains critical financial figures (balances and disbursements) for sub-items b, c, d, and e, as well as sub-bullets for item f. All of this financial data is missing in the output. Second, Item 8 (Funding Committee Report) in the source includes sub-item 'a) FY 2021 Proposed PSAP Distribution (Roll Call Vote Required)……….Marsha Tapler', which is entirely missing from the output list.
checks.b_evidence_source: 5b) NG 911 Fund/Account: April 2020 Month-End Balance $ 66,897,707, April 2020 Disbursement $ 65,036; 8a) FY 2021 Proposed PSAP Distribution (Roll Call Vote Required)……….Marsha Tapler
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "b) NG 911 Fund/Account"; [Item 8a text is completely absent]

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that is present generally matches the source, with punctuation and white-space normalization that is acceptable for structured conversion.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source document. No text blocks or sections appear twice in the output that only appear once in the source. The page headers/footers in the source are correctly excluded from duplication in the semantic structure.
The output uses an H1 for the main 'AGENDA' title and an H2 for the meeting name. This is a logical and accessible hierarchy that reflects the document's structure.

The table structure in the output is corrupted. The source PDF defines a three-column layout (Tab, Topic, Presenter). However, the output table's data rows do not maintain this structure. Some rows have only a single cell that merges all three columns into one string (e.g., 'Call to Order...Eric Boyette'), while others have two cells where the second cell merges the 'Topic' and 'Presenter' columns. This violates the header-data relationship defined in the first row.
checks.b_evidence_source: Source table shows three distinct columns: Tab (numbers), Topic (descriptions with dots), and Presenter (names).
checks.b_evidence_output: Row index 1: { "cells": [ { "cell_type": "data", "content": [ { "text": "Call to Order...Eric Boyette" ... } ] } ] } (1 cell total); Row index 4: { "cells": [ { "text": "1." }, { "text": "Chair's Opening Remarks...Eric Boyette" } ] } (2 cells total).

checks.a_analysis: The reading order generally follows the logical progression of the agenda from top to bottom. While some content is missing (noted in S5), the items that are present are in the correct sequence.

The output fails to preserve the nested list structure and content of the source document. In Item 5, all indented financial data (Month-End Balance, Disbursement figures) for sub-items b, c, d, and e are omitted. In Item 6, the nested list items (i. Monthly Reports, ii. Mitchell County...) under sub-item 'f' are missing. Additionally, on Page 2, sub-item 'a' under Item 8 is entirely missing from the list structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: 5. Consent Agenda... b) NG 911 Fund/Account April 2020 Month-End Balance $ 66,897,707 April 2020 Disbursement $ 65,036; 8. Funding Committee Report... a) FY 2021 Proposed PSAP Distribution (Roll Call Vote Required)..........Marsha Tapler
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "b) NG 911 Fund/Account" (indented data missing); Item 8 is a single item with no sub-item 'a'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 0, element index 4 and 5; Section 1, element index 0

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no interactive links. The output correctly represents the text without injecting or missing any links.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF maintains a consistent, multi-column table layout (Tab, Topic, Presenter) for the entire two-page agenda. The converted HTML output fragments this organization significantly. Items 1-6 are placed in a table, but their sub-items (5a-f, 6a-g) are moved into a separate bulleted list. Items 7-13 are further separated into another bulleted list following a large logo image. This breaks the visual flow and logical grouping of the document, as information that was previously unified in a single table is now scattered across different layout elements.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF (Pages 1-2) shows a continuous table structure with defined columns for 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' spanning from 'Call to Order' through item '13. Other'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows item numbers 1-6 in a table, followed by bulleted lists for sub-items, a large 'NC DIT' logo, and a final bulleted list for items 7-13. The single table has been split into five distinct, unrelated visual blocks.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document; Agenda items section.

checks.a_analysis: The table rendering in the HTML is severely broken. For items 1-6, the sub-items (indented in the PDF) have been stripped from the table cells and placed in a bulleted list below. For items 7-13, the table structure is abandoned entirely, and they are rendered as simple text bullets. This results in the loss of the 'Presenter' column alignment for half the document; for example, the names of the presenters for items 7-13 are appended to the end of the text line rather than being visually distinct in a separate column. Additionally, the financial data (balances and disbursements) under item 5 is not rendered with its original alignment and appears to be missing text values entirely in the screenshot.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF uses a structured table layout where 'Topic' and 'Presenter' are aligned in columns. Item 5 includes detailed financial sub-rows with aligned dollar amounts.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, items 7-13 are rendered as a bulleted list (e.g., 'Education Committee Report...Chuck Greene/Angie Turbeville') instead of a table. The 'Presenter' column information for these items is no longer visually distinct.

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful 'NC DIT' logos present in the source PDF (at the top of page 1 and mid-way through page 2) are present in the HTML output. One logo is positioned between items 6 and 7, and another smaller version appears near the footer, which corresponds to the original document's placement of the logos.

The document content appears unique throughout. The agenda items are listed sequentially without repetition. The 'NC DIT' logo appears multiple times, but this reflects its placement in the original source document at the start of each page section.
